title: No Dockerfile for FabricService
description: Dockerfile template exists in src/Docker.md but it's not a standalone Dockerfile file — lacks requirements.txt and CMD uses uvicorn main:app while main.py does not define app
impact: Cannot deploy fabric in containerized environment; docker build fails without requirements.txt and main:app
proposed_solution: Create actual Dockerfile in root: Python 3.11 slim → install dependencies from requirements.txt → copy src/ and fix CMD to run main.py via asyncio
notes: CMD needs to use python -m main or create an ASGI app object

title: No docker-compose.yml
description: docker-compose.yml template only exists inside src/Docker.md but not as a standalone file in the root — no ability to run dependent services
impact: Integration tests cannot be run and developer needs to manually create full topology
proposed_solution: Create actual docker-compose.yml with fabric-service, redis, optional actor_service

title: No CI/CD Pipeline
description: No GitHub Actions, Jenkinsfile, or any CI exists — no automation for testing or deployment
impact: Any merge happens without tests — zero quality assurance and risk of breaking production
proposed_solution: Create GitHub Actions workflow: lint → typecheck → unit tests → integration tests → build image

title: No Runtime Configuration Schema
description: No schema for YAML configuration — transport_example.yaml is good but any wrong config causes silent runtime failure that cannot be detected before runtime
impact: Configuration errors only detected at runtime with no pre-validation
proposed_solution: Create Pydantic schema for each config and JSON Schema for YAML; add ConfigValidator before load_config

title: Partial Graceful Shutdown / Signal Handling
description: AppRunner in runner.py implements full signal handlers (SIGINT/SIGTERM) with Windows fallback and reverse close via FabricClient.close() — but main.py does not import AppRunner making graceful shutdown inaccessible
impact: Unclean shutdown — data loss, stuck sessions, resource leaks because main.py lacks AppRunner import
proposed_solution: Add from wiring.runner import AppRunner to main.py to link existing implementation to entry point
evidence: src/wiring/runner.py:62-78 implements handlers; src/main.py:23 missing import
notes: runner.py is ready — issue is only in main.py import

title: No Versioning Strategy for Internal APIs
description: No strategy to document and version the ICommunicationGateway contract — no __version__ or CHANGELOG
impact: Contract changes break all consumers without warning or migration period
proposed_solution: Add __version__ to contracts and document breaking changes in CHANGELOG

title: Missing requirements.txt
description: No file to install dependencies — Docker.md assumes requirements.txt exists but the file is completely missing from the project
impact: Prevents final Docker image build, breaks package installation, and breaks any CI/CD environment
proposed_solution: Create requirements.txt listing actual project dependencies (asyncio, PyYAML, etc.)
evidence: Docker.md:9 references COPY requirements.txt but it does not exist

title: Missing pyproject.toml / setup.py
description: No Python package file exists — project cannot be installed as package or deployed or version managed
impact: Cannot pip install -e . or deploy as package or run tests easily with pytest
proposed_solution: Create pyproject.toml with metadata and dependencies using setuptools or poetry

title: Broken AppRunner import in main.py
description: main.py calls AppRunner.run() on line 23 but AppRunner is not imported — causes immediate NameError on execution
impact: Application does not work on run — NameError prevents service startup entirely
proposed_solution: Add from wiring.runner import AppRunner to top of main.py

title: No .gitignore
description: No .gitignore exists — __pycache__ and .pyc files are pushed to repo causing repo bloat
impact: .pyc files and __pycache__ directories appear in repo causing noise in reviews and unnecessary diffs
proposed_solution: Create .gitignore that ignores __pycache__/ and *.pyc and .env and .venv and *.egg-info
evidence: glob results show .pyc files in repo
